title: Living in Boulder vlog 9 - 11 - 2017: No more reefer for me.

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog announcing he has quit marijuana, describing the withdrawal fatigue and low motivation of his first sober days. He talks about reorganizing his house, listening to Herodotus audiobooks, and building a cycling cadence chart for indoor bike training he plans to do daily.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Glad he quit — sobriety will help him breathe better, raise his heart rate during exercise, and stay more motivated
Weed made him driven toward his website but suspects sobriety may push him more toward outdoor activity
Confident he will work on his bike daily and have his website in a presentable place within a year

entities: Herodotus (Herodotus's Secret History / Wars — likely Histories)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is my second time doing this video because the first time had barely any sound.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot to plug in my microphone the first time, which caused the sound issue.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The microphone is only two feet away from the computer, so it picks up computer noise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a v-log for the last few days because I've been busy moving stuff from my mom's basement.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was depressed the first day and slept all day long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had crickets in my Gecko cage but couldn't motivate myself to deal with them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I helped my mother the next day and didn't get any sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I was organizing my house to prepare for writing my bike inside.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I listened to 'Percopia Secret History' and 'Percopia Wars' recently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Being sober will help me listen to books and possibly start a podcast.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm making a black chart for writing my rollers with six different cadence categories.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I created a chart with cadences ranging from 30 to 1680 but got them jumbled up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to redo the 60s, 80s, 90s, 100, and 120 cadences tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The watts didn't appear on my computer while pedaling, possibly due to dead batteries.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll call tomorrow to figure out if the issue is dead batteries.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The chart will be on my bike race level page with links once it's set up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once the chart is ready, I'll start writing my bike inside daily.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I quit smoking partly because I ran out of money but also for health reasons.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't get my heart rate over 130 without risking an asthma attack.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm curious how my personality will change now that I'm sober.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Weed made me super driven on my website, but I might be more driven outside now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow I'll call to fix the bike issue, finish the chart, and start writing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm confident I'll work on my bike all day every day within a year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Within two years, my website will be in a really good place.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-log for September 11th, 2017. It's 1.43 a.m. on September 12th right now.
This is my second time doing this video. It's annoying having to do videos twice.
What happened was I could barely hear myself. There was barely any sound.
I have no idea why. It was the very first time my computer has acted like this.
So then I came back and I forgot to plug in my microphone, so I came back and I plugged in the microphone.
The other time I used the microphone, I suspected that it wasn't working because I could still hear the computer.
But I think what's going on is the microphone is only two feet away from the computer, so it's still picking up the noise from the computer.
But at least I can hear myself.
So let's see, what did I say?
I haven't done a V-log for the last few days because I've just been too busy.
My brother got a house and I had to go over to my mom's house and take all the stuff out of the basement
And help him pack up his U-Haul so I can bring him to his house.
And then, yeah, I just quit smoking weed. I'm totally sober now.
And so I was depressed the first day. I slept all day long.
Like, I had to do stuff.
I had these crickets and I had to put the crickets in my gecko cage and I couldn't even motivate to do that.
I just fell asleep. I slept great.
But that meant that I was up all night the next day.
But then that day I went to help my brother, so I didn't get any sleep.
And then I helped him, so I was able to get to sleep.
Then I got up and I had a good...
Today I got up and I was on diurnal.
And I was basically just organizing my house, trying to get it set up, ready for me to start riding my bike inside.
And for the last few days I was listening to Procopia's Secret History.
And then today I did Procopia's Secret History and then Procopia's Wars.
Like the Vandalic War, Gothic War.
Pretty soon, because I'm sober, I'll be able to listen to these books.
You know, I'll remember everything and then I'll do a video about it every day.
Maybe I'll even start up a podcast.
So, oh yeah, so I'm not riding now because I wanted to...
The other day I'm making a chart, the watt chart, for riding my rollers.
And I did.
There are six different cadence categories I'm going to plug in.
Because those were the cadence categories from this website that I collected.
How fast you go at certain cadences and the different gears.
And I created the gears based on what I have.
And so I did a bunch of them all the way up through 40, 60, 80.
And I uploaded them to Strava.
But then Strava, I didn't know Strava would do this, but I had them all jumbled up.
So I didn't know which was which.
So I knew the 40s, but I wasn't able to do the 60s.
So I'm going to do the 60s, 80s, 90s, 100, 120 tomorrow.
Actually, just now.
But I couldn't because I was pedaling for two minutes.
And the watts never appeared on my computer.
So maybe the battery's dead.
I don't know.
I'm going to call tomorrow.
They'll help me figure it out.
And if it turns out the battery's dead, I'll say, I know what the problem is next time.
So, yeah, so I'm going to be able to make the whole chart tomorrow and then put links to it.
You can still see it.
You can see it now.
It's on my bike racer level.
So you click on sports and then bike racer level and then right there in the upper left, two clicks in.
It has links to the Strava, but only the 40 RPMs work.
And once I have that set up, I'll be able to start riding my bike all day, every day, inside.
And doing V-log, daily V-log, daily V-logs and then request videos and all that good stuff.
Because I'll be more motivated to do that and able to balance myself because I'll be sober.
And I haven't had, I don't have any desire to smoke weed.
The reason that I quit was because I ran out of money.
But also, I mean, you know, if I had plenty of money, I would still be smoking.
And I'd be riding my bike and that would be keeping me from getting asthma.
But also, it's good that I quit because that means I can breathe.
I can't get my heart rate over, like, 130.
That's at least, that's what I figured out when I was running on treadmills before I had an asthma attack.
So, yeah, I need to fix that.
And, yeah, I'm curious to see what my personality would be like when I'm sober.
I mean, I know I'll be more motivated to do all of the videos and more all around with my website.
One thing I'm curious is, will I be more motivated to ride my bike outside?
So, I don't know.
Because the weed makes me super driven on my website.
And if I'm not quite as driven on my website, I might be more, I'll be able to do right outside.
So, anyway, that's what I'm doing tomorrow.
Call Garmin, figure out why it's not working.
Hopefully, get it to work.
And then finish the chart and start riding.
And then I'll get going on my screen captures.
But who knows, man?
I can do whatever.
It's just that the screen captures are the easiest.
But I can say with confidence that, you know, if I work on my bike all day, every day, within a year, I'll have my website in a really, like, a good place.
A presentable place.
And then within two years, it'll be really good.
So, anyways, I think that's enough talking.
And this one was like a minute longer than the other one.
Okay.
Oh, this one was like a minute longer than the other one.
